classFeature.name: Bone Grinding
entries: At 1st level, you learn how to wring magical power from the bones, blood, and tissues of creatures. To use this ability, you must have a {@item bone grinder's kit|IRKCore} or {@item cook's utensils|PHB} in hand and ingredients harvested from a creature's body.

entries.name: Harvesting Ingredients
entries: Harvesting requires the carcass of a Small or larger creature dead no longer than 1 day, a bone grinder's kit or cook's utensils, and 10 minutes of work. Make a Wisdom (Survival) check (DC = 10 + ½ the creature's CR). On failure, the ingredients are destroyed. Ingredients remain useful for {@dice 2d4} days.

entries.name: Creating Talismans
entries: To create a talisman you need the relevant ingredients and tools (a bone grinder's kit is optimal; cook's utensils or sewing supplies can substitute). The description of each talisman lists the necessary ingredients. If a talisman requires a saving throw, use your spell save DC. Unless otherwise noted, any creature that can stomach it may use your talismans.

classFeature.name: Favored Ingredient
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you have studied, tracked, hunted, and processed a certain type of creature. Choose a type of favored creature: beasts, fey, giants, humanoids, monstrosities, or undead.

You gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with weapon attacks against creatures of the chosen type, advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track them, and advantage on Intelligence checks to recall information about them.
When you extract an ingredient from a favored creature, you receive two ingredients rather than one.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have studied the workings of magic and can channel it through manipulated meat and bone. To observers, you appear to be using bone grinder totems rather than casting spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You must have a spellcasting focus—specifically a bone grinder talisman, {@item bone grinder's kit|IRKCore}, or {@item cook's utensils|PHB}—in hand when you cast any spell with this feature.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter bone grinder spell list|spells|class=Bone Grinder}. You learn additional bone grinder cantrips at higher levels (see Cantrips Known in the Bone Grinder table). When you gain a level in this class, you can replace one known cantrip with another from the {@filter bone grinder spell list|spells|class=Bone Grinder}.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Bone Grinder table shows your spell slots. To cast a spell, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare your spells by choosing from the {@filter bone grinder spell list|spells|class=Bone Grinder}. You can prepare a number of spells equal to your Intelligence modifier + half your bone grinder level (rounded down, minimum 1). You can change your prepared spells when you finish a long rest (1 minute of study per spell level for each spell).

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for bone grinder spells.
Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier

entries.name: Ritual Casting
entries: You can cast a bone grinder spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and you have the spell prepared.

entries.name: Talismans
entries: In addition to spells, you learn to create talismans as you gain levels (see Talismans Known in the Bone Grinder table). Talisman rules and recipes appear in the bone grinder class description.

classFeature.name: Tool Expertise
entries: Starting at 2nd level, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using the tool proficiencies you gained from this class.

classFeature.name: Cookbook
entries: At 3rd level, you record talisman formulas in a cookbook. Whenever you finish a long rest and consult your cookbook, you can replace one talisman you know with another talisman available to you.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo this feature to take a feat instead.

classFeature.name: Uncommon Talismans
entries: At 6th level, you gain the ability to craft uncommon talismans.

classFeature.name: Brew Master
entries: At 7th level, if you fail a Wisdom (Survival) check to harvest an ingredient from a creature or to craft a bone grinder talisman, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short rest before you can use it again.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Preservative
entries: At 10th level, you mystically increase the durability of the talismans you carry. While you are wearing or carrying a talisman, it takes twice as long to decay.

classFeature.name: Rare Talismans
entries: At 11th level, you gain the ability to craft rare talismans.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Avid Recycler
entries: At 14th level, you become skilled at repurposing talisman ingredients. As an action, you can destroy a bone grinder talisman to gain a number of ingredients equal to the number required to create that talisman.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Legendary Talismans
entries: At 18th level, you gain the ability to craft legendary talismans.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Talisman Link
entries: At 20th level, you develop a mystical connection to your talismans. You gain a +1 bonus to all saving throws per magic item you are currently attuned to.
In addition, if you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can use your reaction to destroy one of your talismans and drop to 1 hit point instead.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Shamans draw on their faith and connection to the spirits of the world to cast spells. See the {@filter shaman spell list|spells|class=Shaman}.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter shaman spell list|spells|class=Shaman}. You learn additional shaman cantrips at higher levels (see Cantrips Known in the Shaman table).

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Shaman table shows your spell slots. To cast a spell, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots on a long rest.

You prepare your spells by choosing from the {@filter shaman spell list|spells|class=Shaman}. You can prepare a number of spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + your shaman level (minimum 1). You can change your prepared spells on a long rest (1 minute of communing per spell level for each spell).

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for shaman spells.
Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

entries.name: Ritual Casting
entries: You can cast a shaman spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and you have the spell prepared.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a divine focus as a spellcasting focus for your shaman spells.

classFeature.name: Shamanic Domain
entries: At 1st level, choose one domain related to your deity: {@b Matron} or {@b Predator}. Your choice grants you domain spells and other features when you choose it, and additional ways to use Shamanic Rites at 2nd level and further features at 6th, 8th, and 17th level.

entries.name: Domain Spells
entries: Each domain has a list of spells you gain at the shaman levels noted in the domain description. Once gained, a domain spell is always prepared and doesn't count against your prepared spells. If a domain spell doesn't appear on the {@filter shaman spell list|spells|class=Shaman}, it is nonetheless a shaman spell for you.

classFeature.name: Shamanic Rites
entries: At 2nd level, you learn to perform mystic rites. You start with two rite effects: one determined by your Shamanic Domain and one you select from the options below. When you gain a level in this class, you can replace one known rite with another.

When you use this feature, choose which effect to create. You must then finish a short or long rest to use Shamanic Rites again. A creature can only be affected by one rite at a time; a new rite replaces any previous one.
Some effects require saving throws against your shaman spell save DC.
You can use Shamanic Rites once between rests at 2nd level, twice between rests starting at 4th level, and three times between rests starting at 12th level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo this feature to take a feat instead.

classFeature.name: Ritual Protections
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you perform a Shamanic Rite, you gain a bonus to AC equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1) until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Rites of Magic
entries: Starting at 8th level, you can expend one use of Shamanic Rites to cast a spell you have prepared without expending a spell slot.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Ritual Knowledge
entries: At 10th level, you gain a new Shamanic Rite effect of your choice.

classFeature.name: Quick Rites
entries: Also at 10th level, once per short or long rest, when you use a Shamanic Rite that normally requires an action, you can use a bonus action instead; or when you use a Shamanic Rite that uses a reaction, you gain an additional reaction until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Ritual Wards
entries: Also at 10th level, when you use Shamanic Rites, you have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Unbound Rituals
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can expend a spell slot of 6th level or higher to regain one use of Shamanic Rites.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Ritual Knowledge
entries: At 17th level, you gain another new Shamanic Rite effect of your choice.

classFeature.name: Ritual Knowledge
entries: At 18th level, you gain another new Shamanic Rite effect of your choice.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Master of Rites
entries: At 20th level, as a bonus action, you can cause your next rite to have one of the following effects.

items: {@b Extensive Rite.} When you choose a Shamanic Rite effect that targets one creature, you can extend its effects to another creature within range.
{@b Irrefutable Rite.} Creatures have disadvantage on saving throws against your rites.
{@b Prolonged Rite.} You increase the duration of one of your rites to 1 minute.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: At 1st level, you can harness the fury of yourself and the warbeasts you control, weaving it into potent spells.

entries.name: Fury Points
entries: Rather than spell slots, you use fury points. The Warlock table shows your maximum fury points. To cast a warlock spell of 1st level or higher, you must expend fury points equal to the spell's level. You can upcast a spell up to the Maximum Spell Level shown in the table for your level.
You regain fury points in three ways:

items: {@b Dissever.} As a bonus action, you can spend up to a number of Hit Dice equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1), regaining fury points equal to the number of Hit Dice spent. You can only dissever once per long rest.
{@b Forcing.} When you force a bonded warbeast within your control range, you automatically gain fury points equal to those the warbeast gained.
{@b Long Rest.} Each time you complete a long rest, you regain your maximum fury points.

entries: Your control range = your maximum fury points × 10 feet.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: At 1st level, you know one cantrip of your choice from the {@filter warlock spell list|spells|class=Warlock}. You learn additional cantrips at higher levels (see Cantrips Known in the Warlock table).

entries.name: Spells Known
entries: You know one 1st-level spell from the {@filter warlock spell list|spells|class=Warlock}. The Warlock table shows when you learn more spells. A new spell must be no higher than your Maximum Spell Level. When you gain a warlock level, you can replace one known spell with another of an eligible level.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for warlock spells.
Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a rune-inscribed weapon or item as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.

classFeature.name: Warbeast Bond
entries: At 1st level, you gain the ability to mentally bond with certain creatures, commanding them as warbeasts.

entries.name: Bonding
entries: To forge a bond, you must touch a creature you share a resonance with and make a Wisdom (Animal Handling) check contested by the warbeast's Wisdom check. If you win the contest, you attune to the creature's spirit as if attuning to a magic item. If you lose, the creature is immune to your bonding attempts for 24 hours.
Being bonded counts as being attuned to a magic item. At 1st level you can bond to one warbeast; you can bond to one additional warbeast at 4th, 7th, and 10th level.
The total challenge rating of all bonded warbeasts can't exceed half your warlock level (don't round).
Your bond persists until the warbeast dies or you sever it (an action).

entries.name: In Combat
entries: A bonded warbeast acts during your turn. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but can normally only take the Dodge action or make a single attack. A bonded warbeast is always considered a willing creature for your spells unless it is frenzied. If you are incapacitated, each bonded warbeast acts according to its own instincts.

entries.name: Forcing Warbeasts
entries: You can force a bonded warbeast within your control range. Each ability that lets you force a warbeast specifies when and what action it requires. Each time a warbeast is forced, it gains at least 1 fury point. Each day, a warbeast can safely gain fury points up to its threshold; beyond that, further forcing may cause it to frenzy. See Chapter 6 for full warbeast rules.

classFeature.name: Warlock Resonance
entries: At 1st level, choose the type of resonance you have from the types detailed at the end of the class description. Your resonance determines which creatures you share an innate link with and which warbeasts you can bond with. It also grants features at 1st, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th level.

entries.name: Resonance Spells
entries: Each resonance has a list of spells you gain at the warlock levels noted in the resonance description. Once gained, a resonance spell is always prepared and doesn't count against your spells known. If a resonance spell doesn't appear on the {@filter warlock spell list|spells|class=Warlock}, it is nonetheless a warlock spell for you.

classFeature.name: Fury Manipulation
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can spend fury points to boost your combat abilities.

items: {@b Attack Bonus.} Before you make a melee weapon attack, spend 1 fury point to make the attack roll with advantage.
{@b Damage Bonus.} After you make a melee weapon attack, spend any number of fury points to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} weapon damage per fury point.
{@b Shake It Off.} Spend 1 fury point to make a saving throw that could end a condition or effect with advantage.
{@b Warbeast Action.} You can force your warbeast to gain fury points so it can use Attack Bonus, Damage Bonus, or Shake It Off.

classFeature.name: Awaken Animus
entries: At 3rd level, while a bonded warbeast is within your control range, you can cast its animus as if it were a spell you know. The animus doesn't count against your number of spells known.

Additionally, you can force a warbeast to cast its own animus. When you do so, the warbeast gains fury points equal to the spell level of the animus. A warbeast can't cast its animus if doing so would cause it to exceed its threshold.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo this feature to take a feat instead.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Enhanced Control
entries: When you reach 11th level, and again at 13th and 15th level, the maximum challenge rating of warbeasts you can bond to increases by 1.

classFeature.name: Transfer Damage
entries: Starting at 11th level, when you would take damage, you can use your reaction and spend 1 fury point to cause one bonded warbeast to take all the damage and its effects instead. Any damage exceeding the warbeast's current hit points is dealt to you after the warbeast drops to 0.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Reave Fury
entries: Starting at 13th level, as a reaction when a bonded warbeast within your control range drops to 0 hit points, you gain fury points equal to the warbeast's current fury total. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using this feature again.

classFeature.name: Spirit Healing
entries: Starting at 15th level, whenever you cast healing word targeting yourself or a bonded warbeast, you cast it as if it were two spell levels higher than the level you cast it at.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Stir the Spirit
entries: Starting at 17th level, when you cast an animus from one of your warbeasts, you can reduce the fury cost by your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1), potentially reducing the cost to 0. Once used, you must finish a long rest before using this feature again.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: See 4th-level Ability Score Improvement.

classFeature.name: Spirit Bond
entries: At 20th level, at the start of your turn, you can gain 1 fury point for each bonded warbeast within your control range. Once used, you must finish a short or long rest before using this feature again.

classFeature.name: Path
entries: At 3rd level, you choose a path that shapes your bone grinding practice. Choose the Way of Bile or the Way of Bone, both detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 5th, 9th, and 15th level.

classFeature.name: Path feature
entries: At 5th level, you gain a feature granted by your Path.

classFeature.name: Path feature
entries: At 9th level, you gain a feature granted by your Path.

classFeature.name: Path feature
entries: At 15th level, you gain a feature granted by your Path.

classFeature.name: Shamanic Domain feature
entries: At 2nd level, you gain a feature granted by your Shamanic Domain.

classFeature.name: Shamanic Domain feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Shamanic Domain.

classFeature.name: Shamanic Domain feature
entries: At 8th level, you gain a feature granted by your Shamanic Domain.

classFeature.name: Shamanic Domain feature
entries: At 17th level, you gain a feature granted by your Shamanic Domain.

classFeature.name: Warlock Resonance feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warlock Resonance.

classFeature.name: Warlock Resonance feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warlock Resonance.

classFeature.name: Warlock Resonance feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warlock Resonance.

classFeature.name: Warlock Resonance feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warlock Resonance.

subclassFeatures: Might of Blood and Bone|Bone Grinder|IRKBnB|Way of Bone|IRKBnB|3

subclass.name: Judgment Domain
subclassFeatures: Vigilance and Voice of the Law|Cleric|PHB|Judgment|IRKBnB|17

subclassFeature.name: Meat Fueled
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you learn to wring arcane power from any creature's flesh. As a bonus action, you can destroy one or more of your ingredients to cast a prepared spell of 1st level or higher without expending a spell slot. You must destroy a number of ingredients equal to the level of the spell you want to cast.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: What Doesn't Kill You
entries: Starting at 5th level, constant exposure to natural toxins has hardened you. You are immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition and have immunity to poison damage.

Additionally, your body produces toxic sweat. A creature that grapples you or otherwise contacts your skin must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@damage 1d4} poison damage. The damage increases to {@damage 1d6} at 9th level and {@damage 1d8} at 15th level.

subclassFeature.name: Made to Order
entries: At 9th level, you have mastered the art of toxicology. When you roll poison damage for a spell you cast or an attack you make, you can reroll any roll of 1 on the poison damage die, and you must use the new roll even if it is another 1.
Additionally, as a bonus action, you can cause your attacks to ignore one creature's resistance to poison damage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Name Your Poison
entries: At 15th level, your digestive system has become a bubbling brew of noxious bile. As a bonus action, you can consume one ingredient to cast {@spell acid arrow} without expending a spell slot.
You can cast {@spell acid arrow} at higher levels by consuming additional ingredients at a rate of one ingredient per spell slot level above 2nd.

subclassFeature.name: Might of Blood and Bone
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can draw savage power from the creatures you harvest. As a bonus action, you can consume one ingredient to deal an extra {@damage 1d4} weapon damage on your melee attacks for 1 minute. The extra damage increases to {@damage 1d6} at 9th level and {@damage 1d8} at 15th level.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Dismember
entries: Starting at 9th level, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can immediately gain one ingredient from the creature. This ability does not affect constructs.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Meat Shield
entries: Starting at 15th level, when you are targeted by a ranged weapon attack or spell attack, you can choose any creature you can see within 10 feet of you to take the damage dealt by that attack instead.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Cantrip
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you learn your choice of one of the following cantrips in addition to your other chosen cantrips: {@spell chill touch}, {@spell fire bolt}, {@spell ray of frost}, or {@spell shocking grasp}.

subclassFeature.name: Mother's Whisper
entries: At 1st level, you gain proficiency with your choice of Animal Handling, Nature, or Persuasion. You gain advantage on skill checks made with the chosen skill.

subclassFeature.name: Aspect of the Mother
entries: At 2nd level, you learn a new rite from your domain. Choose either The Mother's Balm or The Mother's Wrath.

subclassFeature.name: Mother's Rebuke
entries: Starting at 6th level, your melee attacks count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

subclassFeature.name: Healing Aura
entries: Also at 6th level, whenever you cast a spell of 1st level or higher, you or a friendly creature within 60 feet of you regains hit points equal to {@dice 1d4} + your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Cantrips
entries: Starting at 8th level, when you deal damage with a cantrip, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage.

subclassFeature.name: Mother's Protection
entries: Starting at 17th level, as a bonus action, you can expend a spell slot of 1st level or higher to grant resistance to a damage type of your choice to one willing creature within 30 feet of you.

You can expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher to either affect one additional willing creature or grant one additional type of damage resistance for each slot level above 1st. This damage resistance lasts for 10 minutes.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons, medium armor, and shields.

subclassFeature.name: Devourer's Hunger
entries: Beginning at 1st level, your guiding spirit rewards you when you feast upon a defeated foe. When you reduce a creature other than a construct to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to ritually consume its blood or flesh. If you do so, you gain temporary hit points equal to your shaman level + your Wisdom modifier.
Additionally, your hit point maximum increases by 2 and increases by 2 again whenever you gain a level in this class.

subclassFeature.name: Shamanic Rites: Blood Trade
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can use Shamanic Rites to sacrifice your own blood in return for power from your guiding spirit. As an action, you can deal {@damage 1d6} slashing damage to yourself to regain one expended spell slot. The spell slot's level can be equal to or less than your Wisdom modifier.
You must finish a short or long rest before you can use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@damage 2d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Gift of the Beast
entries: Starting at 17th level, your guiding spirit rewards you with a predator's natural defenses. You gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Shamanic Rites (General Options)
entries: The following rites are available to any shaman as part of the Shamanic Rites feature:

subclassFeature.name: Bestial Connection
entries: At 1st level, {@spell animal friendship} and {@spell speak with animals} are added to your known spells but can't be replaced when you gain a level in this class. At 3rd level, {@spell animal messenger} is also added to your known spells and can't be replaced.
You can cast animal friendship, speak with animals, and animal messenger without spending fury points, but you can only target animals, beasts, and warbeasts with a predatory nature with them.
Additionally, you can bond to one Devourer warbeast without counting the bond against the number of items you can attune to.

subclassFeature.name: Eyes of the Predator
entries: Starting at 1st level, as a bonus action, you can see through the eyes of a beast within your control range. While looking through a creature's eyes, you see whatever it sees and gain the benefit of any senses it possesses.

You can see through the eyes of a bonded warbeast or a creature of the beast type with a CR of half your warlock level or lower. You can see through a creature's eyes for 1 minute or end the effect early as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Hunter
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you or another creature in your battlegroup hits with an attack, the attacking creature can use its reaction to take the Help action targeting you or a creature in your battlegroup. The target has advantage on the next attack roll it makes this turn.

subclassFeature.name: Flurry of Talons
entries: Starting at 10th level, when you force a bonded warbeast to take the Multiattack action, you can spend 1 additional fury point to force the warbeast to make one additional attack during the action.

subclassFeature.name: Wurm's Fangs and Talons
entries: Starting at 14th level, as a bonus action, you can spend 1 fury point to cause the attacks of each bonded warbeast you control to count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage, and to deal an extra die of weapon damage. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Calm the Beast
entries: Starting at 18th level, you have learned to temper your warbeasts' rage. The fury threshold of each warbeast you control increases by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Bestial Connection
entries: At 1st level, {@spell animal friendship} and {@spell speak with animals} are added to your known spells but can't be replaced when you gain a level in this class. At 3rd level, {@spell animal messenger} is also added to your known spells and can't be replaced.
You can cast animal friendship, speak with animals, and animal messenger without spending fury points, but you can only target farrow beasts, hogs, boars, or pigs with them.
Additionally, you can bond to one farrow warbeast without counting the bond against the number of items you can attune to.

subclassFeature.name: Maltreatment
entries: Starting at 1st level, once per long rest, at any time during your turn, you can deal {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage to a bonded warbeast within your control range. If you do so, you gain 1 fury point. Maltreatment does not require an action.

subclassFeature.name: Go Hog Wild
entries: At 6th level, you learn to incite your warbeasts into a wild, frenzied charge. You can spend any number of fury points. For each fury point spent, one bonded warbeast within your control range can use its reaction to move up to its speed toward an enemy it can see.
After each affected warbeast's movement ends, roll a Wisdom (Animal Handling) check with a DC equal to 10 + the warbeast's CR (minimum 1). On a failure, the affected warbeast immediately frenzies.

subclassFeature.name: Save Your Bacon
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can draw on the essence of your warbeasts to reinvigorate yourself. As a reaction or bonus action, you can spend 1 fury point and choose a bonded warbeast within your control range. The warbeast takes your choice of {@damage 1d10}, {@damage 2d10}, or {@damage 3d10} necrotic damage, and you gain temporary hit points equal to the damage dealt.

subclassFeature.name: Pig Out
entries: Starting at 14th level, the weapons of your bonded warbeasts count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
In addition, when one of your warbeasts scores a critical hit, you can spend 1 fury point to force the warbeast to immediately make one additional melee weapon attack.

subclassFeature.name: Squeal It Out
entries: At 18th level, you can goad a bonded warbeast into releasing its animus without gaining fury. One bonded warbeast within your control range immediately casts its animus without gaining fury points but then takes {@damage 1d10} necrotic damage per level of the animus.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Bestial Connection
entries: At 1st level, {@spell animal friendship} and {@spell speak with animals} are added to your known spells but can't be replaced when you gain a level in this class. At 3rd level, {@spell animal messenger} is also added to your known spells and can't be replaced.
You can cast animal friendship, speak with animals, and animal messenger without spending fury points, but you can only target trolls or Tiny creatures with them.
Additionally, you can bond to one trollblood warbeast without counting the bond against the number of items you can attune to.

subclassFeature.name: Blood of Trolls
entries: At 1st level, your bond with your full-blood troll cousins awakens in you the revitalizing power of regeneration. When you force a bonded warbeast within your control range, you immediately regain hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Harmonized Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, when one of your bonded warbeasts hits a creature with an attack, you can spend 1 fury point to force the warbeast. If you do so, you and other creatures in your battlegroup make attacks against that same target with advantage until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Forced Regeneration
entries: At 10th level, you learn to tap into the fury of your bonded warbeasts to empower their natural regeneration. You can force each bonded warbeast within your control range to immediately regenerate hit points equal to its Regeneration trait.
If a bonded warbeast doesn't have the Regeneration trait or can't regenerate due to necrotic damage or another effect, the warbeast regains hit points equal to your proficiency bonus instead.

subclassFeature.name: Miserable Meat Mountain
entries: Starting at 14th level, all your bonded warbeasts gain a +2 bonus to AC against melee weapon attacks, and their attacks count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

subclassFeature.name: Attuned Spirit
entries: Beginning at 18th level, you can cast the animus of one bonded warbeast within your control range without spending fury points. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Goad the Beast
entries: Starting at 3rd level, while raging, you can use your bonus action to have a mount you are riding make an attack.

subclassFeature.name: Out of My Way!
entries: Also starting at 3rd level, while raging, if you move at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then you or your mount hit it with a melee attack on the same turn, the target must make a Strength saving throw with a DC of 8 + your Strength modifier + your proficiency bonus. On a failed save, the target is pushed up to 10 feet away from you and knocked prone. If this movement causes the target to enter a space occupied by another creature, that creature takes damage equal to your Strength modifier + rage damage.

subclassFeature.name: Ride-By Attack
entries: At 6th level, you become skilled at performing brutal attacks on the charge. If you hit a creature with a melee attack while raging, the creature can't target you with opportunity attacks until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature as many times as you can rage per day, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bone Crusher
entries: At 10th level, you master the art of crushing your enemies beneath your mount. While mounted, you can move through a space occupied by Large or smaller creatures if all those creatures are prone. When you enter a prone creature's space, your mount can make an opportunity attack against the prone creature. If the attack hits, the target takes extra bludgeoning damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

If your mount would end its movement in a space occupied by another creature, it stops before entering that space, and you must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or fall off the mount, landing prone in a space within 5 feet of it.

subclassFeature.name: Mortar and Pestle
entries: Starting at 14th level, your size and ferociousness force your enemies to the ground, holding them beneath you as you batter them with a flurry of blows. You can make an opportunity attack against any creature that moves at least 5 feet while within your reach. On a hit, a Medium or smaller creature is knocked prone.

subclassFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: When you reach 3rd level, your path grants you the ability to cast spells. Unlike other barbarians, you can cast and concentrate on spells from the runeshaper spell list while you are raging.
Cantrips. You know two cantrips of your choice from the runeshaper spell list. You learn an additional runeshaper cantrip of your choice at 10th level.

Spells Known of 1st Level and Higher. You know two 1st-level runeshaper spells of your choice. You can learn more runeshaper spells as you gain levels (see table). Each spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots. Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can replace one known spell with another from the runeshaper spell list of a level you can cast.

Spellcasting Ability. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your runeshaper spells. Spell save DC = 8 + proficiency bonus + Wisdom modifier. Spell attack modifier = proficiency bonus + Wisdom modifier.
